title: IDisposables created in a using statement should not be returned
description: "IDisposables" created in a "using" statement should not be returnedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen you use ausingstatement, the goal is to ensure the correct disposal of anIDisposableinstance when the control leaves theusingstatement block.If you return thatIDisposableinstance inside the block,usingwill dispose it before the caller can use it, likely causing exceptions at runtime. You should either removeusingstatement or avoid returning theIDisposablein theusingstatement block.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|T
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect using statements that contain return statements returning the IDisposable variable created in the using block
logic.triggering_conditions: (Method contains using block) AND (Contains return statement) AND (Returned value is the using variable) AND (NOT in excluded contexts)
never_trigger_on: Return of different variables not created in using
logic.reasoning: This rule detects the anti-pattern of returning IDisposable objects from within using statements, which causes the object to be disposed before the caller can use it. Pattern matches using statements with variable declarations followed by return of the same variable. Uses regex backreference to ensure the returned variable matches the using variable. Excludes comments and test code. AST-first approach targeting ForStatement nodes (which includes using statements in the parser).

should_trigger: using (var stream = new FileStream(...)) { return stream; }
using (var conn = new SqlConnection(...)) { conn.Open(); return conn; }
using (var fs = new FileStream(...)) { return fs; }
should_not_trigger: var stream = new FileStream(...); return stream;
using (var temp = new MemoryStream()) { } return result;
